title: Map a non-English-medium framework (domain + language reviewer)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. Widening reach to a non-English-medium framework; standards are referenced by identifier (no relicensing of copyrighted source text) and a bilingual educator reviews each record.
objective: Map a non-English-medium standards framework with a bilingual educator reviewer, referencing standards by identifier.
acceptanceCriteria: Maps a non-English-medium standards framework with a bilingual educator reviewer; review qualification (subject + language) recorded.
The framework passes gate-002 (terms + machine-readable source); identifiers resolve.
Records reference standards by identifier; reproduction only where permitted; provenance complete both sides.
Needs a bilingual educator reviewer (verifiedNeed:false until secured); dataset licensed CC-BY-4.0; DCO signed-off.
